purpose: Incremental source records for GS LinkOps AI database building

records.source_name: KSATlite - Smart, Secure and Scalable Ground Operations
records.category: Commercial GSaaS / smallsat and constellation ground network
records.structured_summary: KSATlite is a fully automated Ground Segment-as-a-Service for small satellites, mega-constellations and multi-mission operations. Public material states support for machine-to-machine interfaces, API-driven scheduling, customer portal visibility, anomaly detection, network-of-networks partner integration, and coverage for LEO orbits through 26 stations and 150+ antennas. Public band coverage includes S-band uplink/downlink, X-band downlink and Ka-band downlink. Antenna range is stated as 3.7 m to 6.1 m. Historical performance is stated above 99.75% globally.

extractable_fields.network_model: network of networks with partner integration

records.confidence_level: High for public network/service model; Medium for exact operational performance until contract-level SLA is confirmed
missing_fields: data delivery protocols and cloud endpoints
records.next_action: Create KSATlite provider profile and use as benchmark template for automated GSaaS capability fields; request commercial capability sheet for per-site and per-band confirmation.

records.source_name: KSAT Digital IF / Virtualized Ground Station Architecture reference
records.category: Virtualized ground station / Digital IF architecture
records.structured_summary: KSAT public navigation exposes Digital IF / Virtualized Ground Station Architecture and cloud integration as new technologies adjacent to KSATlite. This is valuable for GS LinkOps AI because virtualized station architecture implies that RF capture, digitized IF streams, cloud routing, scheduling, and downstream processing may be modeled separately rather than treating a ground station as a single fixed hardware asset.

extractable_fields.modeling_implication: Separate station RF front-end, digitized IF transport, baseband processing, cloud handoff and customer delivery as independent compatibility objects.

records.confidence_level: Medium; public page confirms existence of the technology category, but detailed interfaces require source documents or vendor confirmation
records.next_action: Add a Digital IF object model to GS LinkOps AI and continue searching DIFI/VITA-49, cloud ground-station and virtualized baseband materials.

records.source_name: CCSDS Mission Operations Services / Message Abstraction Layer
records.structured_summary: CCSDS Mission Operations standards define a service-oriented framework for spacecraft and ground mission operations. The core modeling value is the separation of mission operations services, common services, message abstraction layer and transport binding. For GS LinkOps AI this is useful as a neutral schema for system adapters: status parameters, control actions, alerts, orbit vectors, schedules, planning requests, software images, service provider/consumer roles, and transport-independent wrappers for legacy systems.

extractable_fields.architecture_pattern: service-oriented architecture with transport-independent service definitions

records.confidence_level: Medium-High for conceptual object model; implementation requires official CCSDS documents and mission-specific ICDs
missing_fields: official CCSDS document IDs for selected services
implementation binding used by each operator
records.next_action: Use CCSDS MO/MAL as the neutral field taxonomy for GS LinkOps AI adapters; map provider APIs and portals into this model.

records.category: Space-to-ground file delivery / mission data transfer standard
records.structured_summary: CCSDS File Delivery Protocol is a standard protocol for transferring files in space missions, including between spacecraft and Earth. For GS LinkOps AI, CFDP is useful as a standard delivery-method tag and as a field to distinguish raw RF/baseband delivery, frame/packet delivery, file delivery, and cloud/object-storage delivery. It should be tracked separately from commercial customer delivery methods such as SFTP, HTTPS, S3 bucket, cloud object storage or provider portal download.

extractable_fields.standard_role: file transfer protocol for space missions
extractable_fields.delivery_layer_use: spacecraft-to-ground or mission data file transfer

risk_tags: not equivalent to customer-facing cloud/SFTP delivery
records.confidence_level: Medium-High for protocol existence and modeling relevance; operational support must be confirmed per mission/operator
records.next_action: Add CFDP as a delivery protocol option and require provider/mission confirmation before marking it supported.

records.source_name: Lights-Out: An Automated Ground Segment for unstaffed Satellite Operations
records.operator: German satellite communications mission Heinrich Hertz / DLR context
records.category: Automated ground segment / operations automation research
records.structured_summary: This 2025 paper presents an automated ground segment concept used for the Heinrich Hertz satellite mission. It describes automated TTC outside office hours, pre-planned and automatically executed schedules, de-confliction between user mission schedules and main mission schedules, operator-configurable automated monitoring and reaction logic, emergency notification, automated flight operation procedures, and a self-service user portal. The portal allows external payload users to schedule predefined experiments, monitor live execution and access ground-station telemetry/RF test equipment during scheduled experiments. The paper is highly relevant to GS LinkOps AI's AI dispatch, risk control, schedule de-confliction and user portal modules.

extractable_fields.reaction_time_claim: less than 1 minute for some payload reconfiguration use cases

risk_tags: not a GSaaS commercial product sheet
records.confidence_level: High for research concept and field taxonomy; Medium for direct commercial applicability
records.next_action: Convert paper concepts into GS LinkOps AI workflow fields: schedule conflict class, monitoring trigger, automated reaction, operator approval, emergency notification and self-service portal permissions.

records.category: Open ground-station network metadata and scheduling model
records.structured_summary: SatNOGS is an open-source satellite ground-station network ecosystem. Public descriptions identify a network web application for scheduling observations, a crowd-sourced satellite transmitter database available through API, station client software that receives scheduled observations, captures transmissions and uploads results, and open ground-station hardware/software components. Although it is not equivalent to professional X-band payload downlink GSaaS, it is valuable for GS LinkOps AI as an open metadata model for station identity, observation scheduling, satellite transmitter records, station status and community evidence.
risk_tags: must not be mixed with commercial GSaaS availability
records.confidence_level: High for open ecosystem model; Low for commercial station capability
records.next_action: Use SatNOGS only as an open-source metadata and scheduling reference; keep separate from commercial provider capability records.

recommended_next_scan_topics: DIFI Consortium / VITA-49 digital IF standards
ATLAS Space Operations Freedom software and antenna network
Viasat Real-Time Earth technical capability sheets
provider API / portal screenshots and documentation
